result:
Build is green. Per task instructions, I'm not running the program again — the mega-loop will re-test.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1259` — `LuaState::check_stack` contained `debug_assert!(n >= 0, "negative 'n'")`. The C semantics (per `ldo.h:27` macro `luaD_checkstackaux`) explicitly tolerate negative `n` as a no-op because the growth check `if (free <= n)` is false when `n < 0` and `free >= 0`. The triggering site is `do_::pretailcall` (line 843), which forwards `fsize - delta` to `check_stack`. When tail-calling into a smaller function (e.g. the deep recursion `function deep(n) if n>0 then return deep(n-1) else return 101 end end; assert(deep(30000) == 101)`), `fsize - delta` is legitimately negative.

**Fix:** Removed the spurious `debug_assert!(n >= 0)` from `check_stack`. The subsequent `if free <= n` guard already short-circuits the negative case (matching C `luaD_checkstackaux`).

**New state:** Recompiles cleanly with `cargo build -p lua-cli`. Did not re-run the test program (mega-loop will re-test).
